reviews.review_msg: In his videos, he skips important topics despite them being very significant for the course. He doesn't read the PowerPoint before presenting. Teaching yourself.TA is on strike yet he is doing. Your main support for questions is not there, he sent an email to everyone saying do not contact him unless you are sick or in dire need.
very good

Went into this class thinking I was gonna fail and came out with the A- 

Just listen to what he says

Scott might not explain stuff in the best way, but it's important to attend lectures cuz you will get a general sense; coupled with the homework assigned and practice exam and some extra learning, you will be ok. It would be awesome if you read the textbook, but it didn't help me much. Textbook is required for assignments and stat tables though.

Despite the other poor reviews,  Scott is a decent prof, has a good sense of humor, and was helpful when I had questions during or outside of class. However his teaching method is a bit slow-paced and notes can be disorganized at times, attending class is also a must as he goes over examples similar to those that you will find in HW and exams.

Even though he's a bit messy, he's not a bad teacher. I went into the class with no stats/math skills and now nearing the end of the year I've learned so much. Just listen to him and read his notes/textbook, watch the videos and go to the lecture, you will be okay. His tests and HW are not as bad as everyone is saying, and his classes are funny af

He is definitely hard to understand and his notes are messy. He did made us go to campus during the strike which was for sure awkward. He seem like a nice prof. But don't take the class if you aren't willing to put up the extra hours of studying after his lecture.

He is a nice person but he should consider that STAT 201 should the more application-based rather than just solving numericals. 
If you want to score well in his exams, practice the sample exams by him thoroughly as he puts kinda same questions in the exams.

His lectures are garbage, cannot understand anything he says, and he talks very slowly and starts talking about irrelevant things. Tests are mostly multiple-choice, but questions are worded strangely, almost like he wants you to do badly. Have to basically teach yourself everything. Hardly replies to emails.
I found lectures hard to follow and learned more on my own then in class. Also questions on tests are worded weirdly.
I was scared of the bad reviews but he's not that bad. Quite adorable ngl and weirdly funny
Do not take this class with this prof unless you absolutely have to. You will regret it.

One of the funniest profs, definitely explains concepts really well, but the homework is tedious and not helpful at all. The exams have questions  are nothing like the homework questionslecture questions. Have to work extremely hard to do well. The final was way too difficult

Funny guy but not my favourite teacher. His assignments are too long and useless (we had to do roughly 20 end of chapter problems every week). His exams questions are nothing like book problems. We had 2 midterms which were mostly mc's and TF with only one calculation problem. The final exam was extremely difficult but scaled.

She is a nice prof but she isn't clear in her explanations. She frequently revises her notes after using them in lecture. Her assignments are really long and are mostly textbook questions.You will be learning more from the textbook than from her
I really enjoyed his lectures. Great mix of documentaries, and very clear articulation. Interesting.

Interesting topic. Not an Arch major, just an elective. Watched documentaries once a week which were actually helpful. The book was somewhat helpful. Attend lectures, although they may be boring at times. Bob is open to questions about the exams and what not. TA took it a little too seriously though when marking. Good class to learn a bit!

Dr. Rose is basically irrelevant for this class; your TA will be your 1st line of contact and they'll be grading everything you do. Lectures are useless because they focus on the past week's content that you'll have already written a 600-word paper on. SO many assignments and grading criteria are VERY vague (be ready to do whatever your TA says).
boring prof. not a hard class..totally average, i almost fell asleep from boredom. talks about himself a lot. Get a good TA and this class is bearable

I crammed the entire thing overnight and still got a B so... No excuses guys. He's pretty funny from like the 2 times I talked to him. Also you SHOULD keep up with the lectures because there is a research report to write... Since it's his first time hosting 201W some parts were rough and not clear, but he fixed those mistakes.
Dr Leavitt was absolutely phenomenal. Very approachable, kind, respectable, and wants you to succeed. Every assignment and the final is fair. Don't need to read textbook, his slides cover it all.

Dr. Leavitt is awesome. His lectures are entertaining and comprehensive and he posts slides so if you can't attend its fine. We a group project, but the final research report on the group project was individual, so your not totally screwed if you get bad group members. The final was super fair too, he wants students to do well and won't trick you.
Very energetic!!
Have you ever watched a boring tv show and wished it would end? Well, multiply James by 50. He is one of the most boring, opinionated, and awful lecturers at SFU.
Worst notes ever encountered

Not clear, quiet and talks about random and useless info. It would be funny in how bad it was if the class hadn't been worth marks. Difficult to follow for these reasons in an otherwise simple class. She was also late to class a lot.

I can also attest that she is very disorganized and is not a good presenter. She seems nervous about speaking. Although the subject was interesting, her presentation style made it very dull. She goes on long random tangents that are dull and take away from the class. Would recommend other teachers as there are much better ones at sfu.
I absolutely love her teaching style. I will be taking future classes with her.
I totally agree with the last person's rating. Natasha is alright But the T.A's are horrible.
By far one of the BEST Psych professors SFU has. I highly recommand her!!
Awesome, especially for her first semester here!!  The final was very hard but the mid-term was easy so it all equaled out.  Highly recommended.
Her lecture includes everthing you need to know in the index of the textbook
bad prof!
Yaps a lot

avoid avoid avoid!!!!! makes no effort to help or clarify, exams are unclear she is unclear, does not welcome comments, does not welcome anything from anyone sitting farther than three rows back from her - horrible teaching style. I am changing my major because her attitude and teaching of the content made me hate the subject so much

Had her for 103, very different prof for 201.  She is probably one of the easier 201 profs.  Her lectures are all theoretical but then when it comes to the tests you need to know the numbers and derivatives.  Pay attention to the tutorial assignments and practice midterm and you'll do okay. Practice derivatives before you start the class.
Your grade is dependent on your group projects. Pass the exams with flying colours but get stuck with a bad group usually results in a ok grade

Peter was the only professor that was informative and engaging in his lectures. I was never bored for a second and I felt like I was actually prepared for university. I can't wait to take bus 237 with him. Would recommend.
He's not bad; we're just dumb.
Good guy.
Great prof - encourages critical thought and democracy.  Although I heard he was a hard marker - I'm doing ok!
Excellent professor.  He cares about his students.  He encourages critical thought and surprisingly, makes a four hour class enjoyable and exciting!!
